page: OVHcloud a10-90 is a KVM-based GPU-accelerated server designed for parallel processing and compute-heavy workloads. The system is powered by an AMD EPYC 9554 processor containing 60 dedicated physical cores and 60 threads running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. It is equipped with 85.0 GB of system memory, a 400 GB local SSD, and two NVIDIA A10 Ampere GPUs that provide a combined 48 GB of VRAM. Network connectivity is supported by a 16 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression. The server demonstrates exceptional capabilities in large language model inference, achieving top-tier token generation and prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models. Memory performance is highly polarized, showing top-tier bandwidth for small cached blocks but falling to average levels for large uncached blocks. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are enhanced by hardware support for AVX-512 and AES instructions.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the a10-90 is defined by its high compute and accelerator density. However, the hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff: with only 1.42 GB of RAM per core, the system is optimized for compute-bound tasks rather than memory-intensive datasets. This configuration is best suited for workloads such as machine learning inference, parallel software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput database operations.
description: OVHcloud a10-90 is a KVM-based GPU-accelerated server featuring an AMD EPYC 9554 processor with 60 dedicated physical cores running at 3.3 GHz, 85.0 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB local SSD. The hardware is equipped with two NVIDIA A10 GPUs delivering 48 GB of total VRAM, supported by a 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and highly efficient LLM inference processing across small, medium, and large models. While the server provides exceptional compute and parallel processing capabilities, the memory-to-core ratio of 1.42 GB per core represents a potential tradeoff for memory-heavy applications. This configuration is highly suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, parallel software compilation, and high-throughput database operations.
og_description: OVHcloud a10-90 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 60 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 85 GB RAM, 400 GB SSD, and dual NVIDIA A10 GPUs with 48 GB VRAM, delivering top-tier LLM inference and multi-core performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud a10-90 is a GPU-accelerated server with 60 AMD EPYC cores, 85 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA A10 GPUs, optimized for LLM inference and parallel computing.
tagline: A dedicated high-compute server featuring dual hardware accelerators and sixty physical cores optimized for parallel processing and machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA A10 GPUs providing 48 GB of total VRAM for accelerated parallel workloads
Sixty dedicated AMD EPYC 9554 physical cores running at 3.3 GHz
Top-tier performance in LLM inference, multi-threaded decompression, and software compilation
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with 400 GB of local SSD storage
Low memory-to-core ratio of 1.42 GB per core, optimized for compute-heavy tasks
